name: Heath Bar Brownie Sundaes/Caramel Sauce-pt 2
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Butter a 9-inch square baking pan with 2-inch-high sides.
3. Combine butter and both types of chocolate in the top of a double boiler set over simmering water. Stir until melted. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
4. In a large bowl, beat eggs, sugar, and vanilla extract with an electric mixer until slightly thickened (about 1 minute).
5. Beat in the chocolate mixture. Add flour, baking powder, and salt; mix until combined.
6. Stir in chopped Heath candy bars. Pour batter into prepared pan and sprinkle with remaining candy.
7. For a fudgy consistency, bake for about 27 minutes, or until a tester inserted into the center comes out with moist batter still attached. For a cake-like texture, bake until a tester inserted into the center comes out with a few moist crumbs attached (about 35 minutes).
8. Cool completely.
9. Warming: Reheat caramel sauce over low heat.
10. Cut brownies into 9 pieces.
11. Spoon warm caramel sauce onto 8 plates.
12. Place one brownie in the center of each plate (reserve extra for another use).
13. Surround each brownie with three small scoops of ice cream.
